gaps: shared download requires a client-minted signed token (op=sdd) not reproducible from captured traffic — ships as a documented stub
all /svc/ responses are protobuf, decoded by hand-written wire-format readers

novel_features.name: Approval-rate ranking
novel_features.description: Rank shared decks by approval rate (upvotes vs downvotes) with a minimum-vote floor, instead of the raw vote counts the website shows.
novel_features.rationale: Requires computing upvotes/(upvotes+downvotes) across the local catalog snapshot — a quality signal the AnkiWeb SPA never surfaces.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick the highest-quality deck for a topic in one command instead of eyeballing vote counts deck by deck.
novel_features.group: Catalog intelligence the website can't do

novel_features.name: Audio/image coverage filter
novel_features.description: Filter shared decks by whether they include audio or images, critical for language learners.
novel_features.rationale: Filters on the audio/images note-count fields decoded from the protobuf list response; the web UI exposes no such filter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surface only media-rich decks when audio matters (language study) without opening each deck.
novel_features.group: Catalog intelligence the website can't do

novel_features.name: Side-by-side deck comparison
novel_features.description: Compare multiple shared decks in one table: approval rate, note count, audio/image coverage, and freshness.
novel_features.rationale: Joins multiple cached deck rows from the local store — there is no compare view on AnkiWeb or in any other tool.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Decide between near-duplicate decks at a glance instead of flipping between tabs.
novel_features.group: Catalog intelligence the website can't do

novel_features.name: Freshness ranking
novel_features.description: Rank or filter shared decks by last-modified date to surface actively maintained decks.
novel_features.rationale: Orders the local catalog by the decoded modified timestamp; the SPA only shows a date column you can't query.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Avoid stale abandoned decks by finding the ones updated recently.
novel_features.group: Catalog intelligence the website can't do

novel_features.name: New-deck drift watch
novel_features.description: Show shared decks that are new or changed for a search term since your last sync.
novel_features.rationale: Diffs the current catalog result against the last SQLite snapshot — impossible without a local persisted store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Re-run a weekly topic search and see only what's new instead of re-scanning the whole list.

novel_features.name: Owned-deck download drift
novel_features.description: Track download-count changes on the decks you've published, between syncs.
novel_features.rationale: Builds a local time series of your shared decks' download counts; AnkiWeb shows only the current number.
novel_features.why_it_matters: See whether your published decks are gaining traction over time without manual note-taking.

novel_features.name: Discovery briefing
novel_features.description: One digest for a topic: top decks by approval rate, audio coverage, the freshest deck, and how many are new since last sync.
novel_features.rationale: Mechanically composes the ranking, coverage, freshness, and drift primitives into a single agent-friendly summary.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Get a complete read on a topic's deck landscape in one call instead of running four commands.

narrative.headline: The only terminal-native way to search, rank, and download AnkiWeb shared decks — and read your cloud-synced decks — with an offline catalog no other Anki tool has.
narrative.value_prop: Every existing Anki CLI wraps the desktop AnkiConnect add-on; none touches ankiweb.net. AnkiWeb CLI talks directly to the website's service layer, decodes its protobuf responses, and keeps a local SQLite catalog so you can rank decks by approval rate, filter by audio coverage, compare candidates side by side, and watch for new decks since your last sync.
narrative.auth_narrative: AnkiWeb uses a session cookie, not an API key. Run `auth login --chrome` to import your logged-in ankiweb.net session, or set ANKIWEB_COOKIES. Public shared-deck search and info need no login.

quickstart.comment: Search the public shared-deck catalog — no login needed.
Rank results by approval rate to find the best deck.
See full detail and reviews for one deck.
Import your ankiweb.net session for your own decks.
List your cloud-synced decks and card counts.

troubleshoots.fix: Run `ankiweb-pp-cli auth login --chrome` to import your ankiweb.net session cookie, or set ANKIWEB_COOKIES.

troubleshoots.symptom: shared download fails with a token error
troubleshoots.fix: Deck download requires a signed token AnkiWeb mints in-browser; this is a known limitation — download the deck from ankiweb.net directly for now.
An empty search term returns no results; provide a keyword, e.g. `shared search spanish`.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for AnkiWeb CLI when you want to discover, evaluate, or track community shared decks from the terminal or an agent, or to read your cloud-synced decks. It is the right tool for ranking decks by quality, filtering by audio coverage, comparing candidates, and watching for new decks — things the AnkiWeb website cannot do. It is NOT for editing flashcards on your desktop (use AnkiConnect-based tools for that).

recipes.title: Find the best audio-rich Spanish deck
recipes.explanation: Ranks audio-bearing Spanish decks by approval rate and narrows the JSON to just title, approval, and note count.

recipes.title: Compare three anatomy decks
recipes.explanation: One table of approval rate, notes, audio/image coverage, and freshness across the three deck ids.

recipes.title: What anatomy decks are new this week
recipes.explanation: Diffs the current catalog against your last sync and lists only new or changed decks.

recipes.title: Topic briefing
recipes.explanation: Top decks by approval, audio coverage percentage, the freshest deck, and new-since-sync count in one digest.
